title: Roku Q3 2024 Earnings: Strategic Shift
text: Roku delivered Q3 results that surpassed Wall Street expectations, reporting net sales of $1.062 billion, representing 16% year-over-year growth. The company achieved a net loss of 6 cents per share, significantly better than analyst consensus estimates of a 32 cent loss. In a notable move mirroring Netflix's approach, Roku announced it will discontinue quarterly reporting of its streaming households metric starting with Q1 2025 results. The company stated: "we will no longer report quarterly updates on Streaming Households and, by extension, ARPU." Roku explained that the streaming industry landscape has evolved substantially. The company emphasized: "our business has also grown and evolved, and we are now primarily focused on growing Platform revenue and profitability." The metric no longer effectively represents the company's business trajectory, particularly as international expansion dilutes overall ARPU figures. Streaming households reached 85.5 million (up 13% year-over-year). Platform segment revenue reached $908 million (up 15%). Streaming hours totaled 32.0 billion (up 20%). Q4 revenue forecast was $1.14 billion. Going forward, Roku will emphasize streaming hours, Platform segment revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and free cash flow as primary performance indicators.
